entries.title: Transaction system plan review — 17 issues found and fixed
entries.summary: Thorough two-pass review of the Transaction system plan (4 documents) against the actual implemented codebase (RBAC, Organization, Core, Notifications, Users). Found 17 issues: 7 CRITICAL (wrong method args passing UUIDs instead of User objects, non-existent selector methods, treating List[Tuple] as ORM objects, transfer_ownership stub, AUTO_APPROVAL flow broken), 2 HIGH (hardcoded target_type breaks user-to-user flows, initiator_id mismatch in duplicate check), 4 MEDIUM (missing notification types, missing RBAC permissions, missing audit actions, missing serializer field), 4 LOW (conventions). entries.critical: 7 critical issues would have caused runtime errors if implemented as-written: (1-2) MembershipOutcomeHandler passes user_id/created_by_id but RBACService expects User objects, (3) OwnershipOutcomeHandler same issue, (4) VerificationOutcomeHandler uses wrong class name (BusinessService vs BusinessAccountService) AND wrong arg types, (5) _validate_creator_authority calls non-existent get_by_id_or_none() and treats List[Tuple] as objects, (6) transfer_ownership() is a NotImplementedError stub, (7) AUTO_APPROVAL transactions stuck in PENDING forever.

entries.title: Transaction system fully implemented with 343 tests, 95.8% coverage
entries.summary: Full implementation of the Transaction system — a unified state-machine engine for invitations, requests, approvals, and ownership transfers. 4-phase build: Pre-Phase (6 notification types, transfer_ownership impl, 7 audit actions), Phase 1 (app structure, constants, types with 10 transaction types, models with CheckConstraint/6 indexes, managers with TransactionQuerySet), Phase 2 (selectors, policies with 4 approver policies, services with dual-authority validation, outcome handlers with pluggable registry, 2 RBAC permissions seeded), Phase 3 (serializers, 8 API views, 4 Celery tasks, rate limiting). Comprehensive test suite: 343 tests across 11 files (models 58, constants 32, types 76, selectors 30, policies 37, services 61, outcome_handlers 11, views 22, tasks 11, rate_limits 5). Fixed 10 bugs found during testing: lazy-import mock paths (5), missing base_member_role in accept fixtures (3), SQLite UNION ORDER BY incompatibility (1), notification type sets in existing tests (3). Transaction app coverage: 95.8%. Updated notification tests for 6 new types. Total suite: 2168 tests, 0 failures, 3 skipped (Redis-only).
entries.critical: SQLite does not support ORDER BY in subqueries of UNION/compound statements. Fix: strip ordering from QuerySets before .union(), then apply ordering on the outer result. Pattern: qs1.order_by().union(qs2.order_by()).order_by('-created_at'). Also: when mocking lazy imports (from X import Y inside a function body), mock at the source module (X.Y) not the consumer module.

entries.title: Form Builder plan review — 33 issues found and fixed in 2 passes
entries.summary: Thorough two-pass review of the Form Builder System implementation plan (3400+ lines) against the entire codebase. Pass 1 found 22 issues (4C/4H/10M/4L): RBAC stub crashes, non-existent methods, empty policies, wrong imports. All fixed. Pass 2 re-verification with 5 parallel agents found 11 more issues: models missing custom manager declarations (FormTemplateManager/FormResponseManager), FormResponse.__str__ lazy FK queries, _create_new_version missing created_by/updated_by, publish_form/archive_form missing updated_by, managers.py missing ResponseStatus import, IndexService wrong StorageType import source, Section 9.1 missing UUID import, dependencies table referencing non-existent PermissionSelector, context capture example outdated, factory docstring key count wrong. All 33 total issues now fixed. Final grep verification: 0 get_membership_from_request, 0 PermissionSelector in code, 0 form_id:str, 0 response_id:str, 0 self_projects paths, 0 apps.core.models.managers, 0 bare pass in policies.
entries.critical: Models missing manager declarations would have caused AttributeError at runtime: FormTemplate.objects.active(), FormTemplate.objects.public_templates() etc. would fail because AuditModel's SoftDeleteManager doesn't have these methods. Fixed by adding objects=FormTemplateManager() and objects=FormResponseManager() to model classes, plus importing managers in models.py.

entries.critical: Custom managers with overridden get_queryset() do NOT auto-proxy QuerySet methods. Must explicitly add delegation methods (e.g., def public_templates(self): return self.get_queryset().public_templates()). Also: soft_delete() only saves is_deleted/deleted_at/deleted_by — any other field changes must be saved separately before calling soft_delete().

entries.critical: Key bugs found: (1) PositiveIntegerField reorder cannot use negative values — use offset 100000+. (2) DRF URLPathVersioning intercepts URL kwargs named 'version' — never use 'version' as a URL param name. (3) soft_delete(user=) not soft_delete(deleted_by=). entries.title: API Integration Tests — 9 runs to 264/0/12 (pass/fail/skip), 1 production bug fixed
entries.summary: Executed 276 API integration tests across 9 iterative runs against Docker PostgreSQL 17 + Redis 7. Progression: Run 1 (44p/177f), Run 2 (160p/75f), Run 3 (172p/70f), Run 4 (175p/64f), Run 5 (184p/53f), Run 6 (195p/43f), Run 7 (247p/17f), Run 8 (259p/5f), Run 9 (264p/0f/12s). Found 1 new production bug: auth_service.py select_for_update() combined with select_related('session') on nullable FK causes NotSupportedError on PostgreSQL (token refresh completely broken on PG, only worked on SQLite). Key test fixes across runs: RBAC scope 'platform' → 'platform_only', CMS page publish/unpublish/export require ?site= param, CMS block schema format is {fields:[...]} not JSON Schema, API key header is X-CMS-API-Key not X-API-Key, API key allowed_origins causes 403 without Origin header, transaction list filtering by role=target for invitation targets, dismiss only works on REQUEST mode transactions. 12 skipped tests: 3 require Celery worker, 6 cascading from earlier skips, 3 require seeded notification types. 2 application gaps documented: duplicate membership invitations allowed, suspended members can view platform profile. Comprehensive test execution report created.
entries.critical: Production bug: select_for_update() + select_related() on nullable FK causes NotSupportedError on PostgreSQL. Token refresh was completely broken on PG — only worked on SQLite because SQLite ignores select_for_update(). Fixed by removing 'session' from select_related().

entries.title: API Integration Tests Session 4 — fixed all 12 skips + 1 application gap, 2592 unit tests pass
entries.summary: Fixed all 12 skipped tests and 1 application gap from Run 9. Root causes (corrected from initial analysis — none were Celery-related): (1) Rate limiting: LoginRateThrottle 5/min and PasswordResetRateThrottle 3/hr exhausted during test sequence → overrode throttle rates in local_docker.py (100/min, 100/hr). (2) A13 rewrite: incomplete stub → proper JTI blacklist verification (login, save token, logout_all, verify 401, re-login). (3) N02 response key: 'results' → 'types' (endpoint returns {types:[...]}). (4) T15 rewrite: system-business-verification form requires form_response_id → created via DBHelper.create_system_form_response() with direct SQL (system-owned forms can't be accessed via API — get_membership_or_403 fails for owner_type='system'). Fixed T16/T18 field names to match actual seed migration fields. (5) F17 user: Carol (denied invitation) → Bob (accepted, is member). (6) WF4 rewrite: fresh user → Alice (platform owner), fixed block template schema format, API key header, publish/unpublish query params. (7) Gap 1 closed: added membership existence check in TransactionService.create_invitation() — only for membership invitation types (not ownership transfers, which legitimately target existing members). Discriminates via outcome_handler string containing 'MembershipOutcomeHandler.handle_invitation_accepted'. Unit test added. Full unit test suite: 2592 passed, 3 skipped (Redis-only), 0 failures. Ready for Run 10 integration testing.
entries.critical: Membership check in create_invitation() must discriminate by outcome_handler — ownership transfers legitimately target existing members. Initial implementation broke WF6 (ownership transfer). Fixed by checking if outcome_handler contains 'MembershipOutcomeHandler.handle_invitation_accepted' before applying the check.

entries.title: API Integration Tests Run 10 — 275 passed, 0 failed, 1 skipped + Bug 5 fixed
entries.summary: Executed Run 10 of API integration tests against fresh Docker PostgreSQL 17 + Redis 7. Results: 275 passed, 0 failed, 1 skipped (A27: requires 2+ concurrent sessions). All 12 previous skips now pass. Found and fixed Bug 5: preference_service.py referenced AuditLog.Action.PREFERENCE_UPDATED (doesn't exist) — correct enum is NOTIFICATION_PREFERENCE_UPDATED. Unit tests masked this by monkey-patching the alias. Caused HTTP 500 on PATCH /notifications/preferences/<type>/. Also updated CLAUDE.md to mandate Docker (PostgreSQL + Redis) as default dev environment — SQLite no longer default. Updated project memory with same rule. Test execution report updated with Run 10 final results.
entries.critical: Production Bug 5: AuditLog.Action.PREFERENCE_UPDATED doesn't exist — correct name is NOTIFICATION_PREFERENCE_UPDATED. Unit tests passed because they monkey-patched the missing attribute. Integration tests (real server) exposed the 500 error. Pattern: monkey-patching missing enum values in tests can mask production bugs.

entries.title: Frontend comprehensive deep review v1
entries.summary: Comprehensive deep review of the entire frontend application (Next.js 16 + React 19 + Tailwind v4 + shadcn/ui). Health check: 62/62 tests passing, TypeScript clean, ESLint clean. Identified 6 bugs (BUG-F01 through BUG-F06): double toast on mutation errors, middleware route matching too broad, callbackUrl not consumed after login, silent refresh not resetting session cookie, resend ignoring user-typed email, logout-all label mismatch. Documented missing test coverage (AuthInitializer, middleware, api-client interceptors are highest priority). Catalogued all placeholder/incomplete areas. Completed Frontend Foundation Overhaul: 7-phase implementation covering bug fixes (6 bugs), membership types/stores/permission hooks, centralized error handling, route guards with 3-tier authorization (AuthGuard, BusinessGuard, PlatformGuard, AdminGuard), app layout with sidebar navigation, API module system (users/business/platform), CSP security headers, and comprehensive tests. 131 tests passing across 20 files (69 new tests). Zero TypeScript errors.

Created comprehensive Frontend Foundation implementation document with developer guide. Covers 21 sections: architecture, 3-tier authorization model, data layer, types, state management, hooks, components, error handling, routes, middleware, security, key flows, backend changes, testing (131 tests), file summary (53 new + 26 modified), gotchas, reusable patterns, limitations, and roadmap.

Added configurable REFRESH_TOKEN_COOKIE_SAMESITE setting (base.py + production.py env var override) for flexible deployment: Vercel + separate backend (SameSite=None) or same-server nginx (SameSite=Strict). Updated implementation doc with deployment topology, new gotcha, and changelog v1.1.
entries.critical: Next.js rewrite proxy `:path*` strips trailing slashes — Django POST endpoints break. Use direct cross-origin API calls with CSP + CORS instead.

entries.critical: TypeScript interface doesn't satisfy Record<string, T> constraint — use type alias instead. Platform RBAC has NO Base Member role (only Owner, Admin, Global Moderator).

entries.title: Business & Platform Profile Pages — 142 new tests, 415 total frontend tests
entries.summary: Implemented business and platform profile pages (public discovery + console management). 4 routes: /business/[slug] (public discovery), /platform/profile (public), /bconsole/[slug]/profile (console with edit/read-only via <Can>), /pconsole/profile (console with edit/read-only via <Can>). entries.title: Explore System — Null profile fix + implementation documentation

entries.critical: DRF nested serializer with read_only=True renders null when reverse OneToOneField has no related object. Always make profile types nullable and guard in components.

entries.title: Profile field wiring — backend service fixes + frontend city/tags in edit forms
entries.summary: Fixed 500 Internal Server Errors on PATCH /users/me/profile/ and PATCH /business/{slug}/ and PATCH /business/{slug}/profile/. Root cause: new model fields (bio, country, city, tags) were added to models, serializers, and views but NOT to service layer methods. Services rejected unknown kwargs via **validated_data unpacking → TypeError → 500. Fixed UserService.update_profile() (+4 params: bio, country, city, tags), BusinessAccountService.update() (+1 param: city), BusinessProfileService.update() (+1 param: tags). Frontend: added Location card (read-only country + editable city ComboboxField) and Tags section (FormTagInput) to BusinessProfileEditForm. Implemented dual-mutation submit pattern — profile fields → updateBusinessProfileApi, account fields (city) → updateBusinessApi via Promise.all. Added useUpdateBusiness mutation hook. 12 new BusinessProfileEditForm tests. 2863 backend tests, 472 frontend tests passing.
entries.critical: Adding fields to Django models + serializers is NOT enough — the service layer methods must also accept the new params. Views use **serializer.validated_data to call services; missing service params cause TypeError → 500.

(2) Invisible transactions: fixed TransactionSelector to find membership_actor-initiated transactions by looking up user_id in initiator_context JSON. (3) Quota enforcement: updated _check_member_quota to count pending membership transactions (invitations + requests) in addition to active members. details: Email search: Added 'email' to SearchVector (weight A) and TrigramSimilarity in ExploreSelector.search_users(). Added exact email match shortcut when query contains '@'. Exposed email field in ExploreUserOutput serializer. 2 new backend tests (FTS + exact match)

Invisible transactions: Root cause — bconsole invitations use initiator_type='membership_actor' with user_id in initiator_context JSON, but list_for_user only matched initiator_type='user'. Added _user_as_initiator_q() helper using Q objects to match both types. Fixed get_initiator_name serializer for membership_actor. 3 new backend tests

Quota enforcement: _check_member_quota now counts non-terminal membership transactions (invitations + requests) using Transaction.objects.filter(transaction_type__contains='membership').exclude(status__in=TERMINAL_STATES). Total committed = active_members + pending_transactions

Frontend rewrite: InvitationCreateDialog now fetches existing members on open (marks as unselectable with 'Member' badge), counts pending transactions for quota, shows quota warning banner when full, disables search when quota full, maps backend errors to user-friendly messages. Added maxMembers prop wired from membership store through wrapper components

entries.critical: Transactions created from bconsole have initiator_type='membership_actor' (not 'user') with user_id stored in initiator_context JSON. Selectors must use Q(initiator_context__user_id=str(user_id)) for JSON lookup to include these in user transaction lists.

entries.title: Fix 409 on re-invitation after member removal + implement form-before-accept workflow
entries.summary: Fixed two critical transaction bugs: (1) Re-inviting a removed member caused 409 ConflictError because create_membership() found the REMOVED membership and treated it as a duplicate. Fix: reactivate REMOVED memberships instead of raising ConflictError. (2) Implemented form-before-accept workflow — when a TransactionFormMapping is configured as required, the accepting user now sees a form dialog, fills it, and the form response is linked to the transaction before acceptance. 833 frontend tests + 661 backend tests (RBAC+Transaction) passing.
details: Bug 1 — 409 on re-invitation: update_membership_status(new_status=REMOVED) only sets status=removed but keeps is_deleted=False. get_membership_for_user_account uses SoftDeleteManager (filters is_deleted=False) but NOT status, so REMOVED memberships are still found. Fix in create_membership(): check existing.status == REMOVED → reactivate with new role, clear status fields. SUSPENDED/BANNED still raise ConflictError. 2 new tests.

Bug 2 — Form-before-accept backend: Added get_form_mapping_for_transaction() selector. Added form_mapping field to TransactionOutputSerializer (returns {id, form_template_id, form_template_name, is_required}). Extended AcceptTransactionInputSerializer to accept form_response_id. In TransactionService.accept(): links form_response_id to transaction if provided, validates is_required form mapping has form response before accepting.

Bug 2 — Form-before-accept frontend: Added TransactionFormMappingEmbed type and form_mapping field to TransactionDetail. Updated AcceptInput to include form_response_id. Created AcceptWithFormDialog component (loads form template fields, renders inputs, creates+submits FormResponse, then calls accept with form_response_id). Wired into TransactionDetailPage: handleAccept() checks needsFormBeforeAccept, opens dialog if needed. Added amber notice card for required form.

Also fixed: TransactionSettingsPage was not sending account_type/account_id in CreateFormMappingInput (400 error when attaching forms). Added cancel button on TransactionCard for pending invitations in list view.
entries.critical: REMOVED memberships (status=removed, is_deleted=False) are visible to get_membership_for_user_account() because SoftDeleteManager only filters is_deleted, not status. create_membership() must explicitly handle REMOVED status by reactivating instead of raising ConflictError.

entries.title: Implement PENDING_REVIEW transaction status + PENDING_APPROVAL membership status for two-phase form acceptance
entries.summary: Implemented two-phase acceptance flow for form-based invitations/requests. When a user accepts a transaction with a required form, instead of immediately becoming ACTIVE, they get a PENDING_APPROVAL membership and the transaction moves to PENDING_REVIEW. Business reviewers can then approve (→ACTIVE), deny (→revoke membership), or request info (→user edits form, resubmits back to PENDING_REVIEW). Backend: 13 new tests. Frontend: 43 new tests (838 total across 103 files). Full backend transaction+RBAC suite: 677 tests passing.
details: Backend — New constants: PENDING_REVIEW in TransactionStatus, PENDING_APPROVAL in MembershipStatus. PENDING_REVIEW transitions to: ACCEPTED, DENIED, CANCELLED, EXPIRED, INVALIDATED, INFO_REQUESTED. INFO_REQUESTED→PENDING_REVIEW for invitation resubmit

Backend — TransactionService.accept() forks: WITH form → creates PENDING_APPROVAL membership + sets status=PENDING_REVIEW; WITHOUT form → creates ACTIVE membership + status=ACCEPTED (unchanged). New approve_pending_review() method activates membership + sets status=ACCEPTED
Backend — deny/cancel from PENDING_REVIEW: soft-deletes the PENDING_APPROVAL membership. request_info from PENDING_REVIEW: sets INFO_REQUESTED. resubmit back to PENDING_REVIEW (for invitations)

Backend — TransactionPolicy: added can_approve_pending_review() (requires can_approve_membership_request permission). get_viewer_permissions() returns can_approve boolean. New ApproveTransactionReviewView at POST /transactions/<id>/approve/
Backend — PENDING_APPROVAL memberships count toward member quota in count_active_members(). create_membership() accepts optional status parameter

Frontend — Added pending_review to TransactionStatus type, can_approve to TransactionPermissions, pending_approval to MembershipStatus. Status configs added to both transaction-statuses.ts and member-statuses.ts
Frontend — New approveTransactionApi() function + useApproveTransaction() hook. ActionButtons.tsx gains Approve button gated by can_approve permission

Frontend — TransactionDetailPage: PENDING_REVIEW banners for reviewer ('Review the submitted form response below') and submitter ('Waiting for the business to review your submission'). handleApprove wired with success/error toasts

Frontend — BusinessGuard + PlatformGuard: find memberships with active OR pending_approval status. Show 'Pending Review — Your membership is pending document review' card for pending_approval users instead of Access Denied
Frontend — Fixed MEMBER_STATUS_CONFIG missing 'left' key (TypeScript Record completeness)
entries.critical: PENDING_APPROVAL memberships count toward member quota. Guards must find both 'active' and 'pending_approval' memberships but render different UI for each. TransactionFormMapping is_required determines whether accept() creates provisional (PENDING_APPROVAL) or immediate (ACTIVE) membership.

entries.title: FormBuilder field validation improvements — client-side + backend validation for all 22 field types
entries.summary: Deep audit of FormBuilder field components found gaps in validation and rendering. Created client-side field-validation utility (email/URL/phone regex, numeric bounds, date ranges, file size/type, option validation, text length/pattern) and backend field-type validators for all 22 field types. Added FileUploadField component with drag-and-drop, preview, and size/type filtering. Fixed isEmpty bug where non-string values (arrays, numbers, booleans, File objects) were always treated as empty, bypassing validation. Frontend: 1008 tests across 105 files (+213 tests, +6 files). Backend forms app: 461 tests (+247 tests). All tests passing.
entries.critical: isEmpty bug: non-string values (arrays, numbers, booleans, File objects) were always treated as empty due to string-only check, bypassing all field validation. Backend validate_field_values() is called in all 3 submission paths: submit_response(), create_and_submit(), update_after_info_request().

entries.title: Public endpoints + open_member_request feature flag — full-stack implementation
entries.summary: Two related features: (1) Made business and platform detail endpoints publicly accessible (AllowAny) so anonymous users can view public profiles. (2) Added open_member_request boolean flag to BusinessAccount and PlatformAccount that controls whether external users can send membership requests. Full-stack: backend model+migration, serializers, services, enforcement in TransactionService, admin, policies, factories, frontend types, RequestToJoinButton component. Backend: 27 new tests (6 anonymous views + 9 open_member_request + 12 existing test fixes). Frontend: 8 new tests + 10 test files updated for new fields. All 1016 frontend tests + 618 backend (transaction+org) tests passing.
details: Phase 0 — Public endpoints: Changed permission_classes from [IsAuthenticated] to [AllowAny] on BusinessDetailView and PlatformAccountView. Updated BusinessPolicy.can_view() + can_view_profile() to allow anonymous access for public profiles. PlatformPolicy.can_view() always returns True
Phase A — Model: Added open_member_request BooleanField(default=False) to BusinessAccount and PlatformAccount. Migration 0005

Phase B — Serializers: Added open_member_request to BusinessAccountOutput, BusinessAccountListOutput, PlatformAccountOutput, ExploreBusinessOutput. Added to BusinessUpdateInput and PlatformSettingsUpdateInput
Phase C — Services: BusinessAccountService.update() handles open_member_request toggle. PlatformAccountService.update_settings() handles the flag

Phase D — Enforcement: New _check_open_member_request() in TransactionService. Called in create_request() BEFORE _check_member_quota(). Raises BusinessRuleViolation(rule='member_requests_closed') when flag is False
Phase E — Admin: list_display, list_filter, list_editable, fieldsets, bulk actions (enable/disable_member_requests) on BusinessAccountAdmin. PlatformAccountAdmin updated
Phase F — Factories: open_member_request=False on both factories. Transaction conftest business fixture set to open_member_request=True to avoid breaking existing tests

Phase G — Frontend types: Added open_member_request: boolean to BusinessAccount, BusinessAccountList, PlatformAccount (organization.ts) and ExploreBusiness (explore.ts). Added to UpdateBusinessData in business-api.ts

Phase H — RequestToJoinButton: New component with 3 guard conditions (not authenticated, requests closed, already member). Uses useCreateRequest mutation with business_membership_request type. Integrated into BusinessDiscoveryPage
Phase I — Frontend tests: 8 new tests for RequestToJoinButton (renders, hidden states, API call, toasts, loading). Updated 10 existing test files with open_member_request field in mock data
Phase J — Test fixes: Updated 3 platform tests for new public access (expected 200 not 401, can_view=True for anonymous). Fixed transaction conftest + quota test to include open_member_request=True
entries.critical: Enforcement ordering matters: _check_open_member_request() runs BEFORE _check_member_quota() — closed requests get rule='member_requests_closed', not 'member_quota_exceeded'. Transaction conftest business fixture must have open_member_request=True to avoid breaking existing create_request tests.

entries.title: BUG-009: Cross-type transaction conflict guard + _relationship injection
entries.summary: Fixed BUG-009: invitation + request for same user+business could coexist, creating duplicate membership risk. Implemented centralized conflict groups on TransactionTypeConfig, cross-type conflict detection in selectors/services, RelationshipInjectMixin for entity detail responses, and smart frontend components using _relationship data. 19 new backend tests + fixed 3 pre-existing frontend test issues. Backend: 1414 tests passing. Frontend: 1078 tests across 109 files passing.
details: Phase 1 — Conflict groups: Added conflict_group field to TransactionTypeConfig dataclass. Groups: business_membership (invitation+request), platform_membership, business_ownership, platform_ownership. get_conflict_group_types() helper returns all types in a group

Phase 2 — Selector: TransactionSelector.has_active_in_conflict_group() queries across all types in a conflict group for (user_id, context_type, context_id). Reuses _user_as_initiator_q() for both user and membership_actor matching
Phase 3 — Service guards: create_invitation() and create_request() check cross-type conflicts when config.conflict_group is set. Raises ConflictError(conflict_type='cross_type_duplicate')

Phase 4 — RelationshipInjectMixin: New mixin in apps/core/views.py (parallel to PermissionInjectMixin). Injects _relationship dict into authenticated GET detail responses. Shape: { membership_status, active_transaction: { id, type, status, mode } | null }
Phase 5 — Business/Platform views: Added _relationship to BusinessDetailView, BusinessByIdView, PlatformAccountView. Helper functions _build_business_relationship() and _build_platform_relationship()
Phase 6 — TransactionListSerializer: Expanded with initiator_type, initiator_id, target_type, target_id for frontend cross-referencing
Phase 7 — Frontend types: BusinessAccountWithRelationship, PlatformAccountWithRelationship, EntityRelationship, ActiveTransactionSummary. TransactionListItem expanded
Phase 8 — RequestToJoinButton rewrite: Uses _relationship from business detail (1 data source vs 3). Shows Request/Cancel/Pending Invitation based on _relationship.active_transaction.mode
Phase 9 — BusinessDiscoveryPage: Removed fetchMyMembershipsApi() refresh effect and membershipsReady state
Phase 10 — InvitationCreateDialog: Extracts user IDs from pending transactions (target_id for invitations, initiator_id for requests). Shows Pending Invitation/Pending Request badges on disabled rows

Phase 11 — Fixed 3 pre-existing test issues: AccountSwitcher.test.tsx and SettingsPage.test.tsx used bare render() instead of renderWithProviders() (QueryClient missing), InvitationCreateDialog.test.tsx payload assertion didn't account for auto-role-selection
entries.critical: Conflict groups are extensible — adding a new transaction type to an existing group (e.g., business_membership) automatically enables cross-type conflict detection. TransactionService.cancel() takes (transaction_id, actor_context) not (transaction, actor_user_id). ActorContext.for_user_context() is in apps.core.types.

entries.title: BUG-010/011/012: Final 3 E2E bugs resolved — zero open bugs
entries.summary: Fixed last 3 LOW-severity E2E bugs. BUG-010: TransactionFormPanel query timing race — removed internal query, fields passed as prop from parent. BUG-011: Transaction Settings permission gap — added useHasPermission Tier 2 guard + backend GET form-mappings permission check. BUG-012: FormBuilder multi-tab state loss — CSS hidden class instead of unmounting non-active tab fields + auto-navigate to first error tab on submit. All 1083 frontend tests + 451 transaction backend tests pass. E2E report: 147 PASS / 0 BUG.
entries.critical: GET /form-mappings/ now requires can_configure_transactions permission (was previously unauthenticated list). Existing test updated from owner_with_invite_perm to owner_with_configure_perm fixture.

entries.title: Chat System description document created
entries.summary: Created comprehensive description document for the Chat System - a scope-isolated, reusable messaging engine. Documents scope isolation model, entity participation rules, conversation types, participant identity model, entity delegation with audit trail, scope-context integration, membership lifecycle impact, and key distinctions between chat groups and teams.

entries.title: Network App implementation — follow/connection management system
entries.summary: Implemented the 9th Django app (apps.network) for follow and connection management. Created Follow (one-way: User→Business/Platform) and Connection (two-way: User↔User, Account↔Account) models with soft-delete and audit trails. Implemented 2 selectors (7+9 methods), 2 services (3+5 methods), and NetworkPolicy with 9 permission checks. Added 4 new transaction types + 2 modified existing types. Wired 13 API endpoints, _relationship injection on detail views, Tier 1.5 permissions, and 5 social notification types. Created FollowOutcomeHandler + ConnectionOutcomeHandler. Added 2 RBAC permissions with data migration. Frontend types/hooks prepared. 92 new tests added. Total: 3394 passing tests (up from 3134).
entries.critical: Follow is directed (User→Business/Platform); Connection is bidirectional (both directions required for active state). FollowOutcomeHandler replaces transaction app stub—must handle accept/deny for 2 new types (business_follow_approval_request, platform_follow_request). ConnectionOutcomeHandler handles 2 new types (business_connection_request, platform_connection_request). Tier 1.5: _relationship injected on Business/Platform/User detail GET, contains membership_status + active_transaction. New permissions: can_manage_followers, can_manage_connections. Data migration 0008 required to seed into Permission table.

entries.title: Network App — compliance audit, 5 fixes, and implementation documentation
entries.summary: Performed comprehensive compliance audit of Network App against plan (96.2% initial alignment). Fixed 5 findings: (M1) user_connection_request resubmission_cooldown_days 3→7, (X1) added followee_name to FollowOutput, (X2) added followee_slug to FollowingOutput, (X3) added name to AccountConnectionOutput.other_account, (X4) added notification dispatch in remove_follower(). Added 3 polymorphic name/slug resolver helpers to serializers.py. Created implementation reference document. All 92 network tests pass, 3405 total tests pass. Post-fix compliance: 100%.

entries.title: Content Visibility System — 3-tier profile privacy with field-level access control
entries.summary: Implemented 3-tier content visibility system (T1=always public, T2=configurable, T3=members-only). Created apps/core/visibility/ package: enums (4 visibility hierarchies), registry (4 field registries with 40+ field configs), resolver (ViewerAccess dataclass + VisibilityResolver with short-circuit DB queries), serializer mixin (VisibilityAwareSerializerMixin). Applied to User profiles (connection-based private access), Business profiles (follower/member access + T2 contact field filtering + T3 legal field RBAC gating), and Explore system (include_private discovery, is_public in outputs, email removed). Added visibility settings API (GET/PATCH) for business and user profiles. 3 model migrations (visibility_overrides JSONField) + 1 RBAC migration (can_view_legal_info permission). 132 new tests, 3525 total pass.
entries.critical: VisibilityAwareSerializerMixin uses visibility_registry class attribute (NOT context) to resolve field registry — each nested serializer has its own registry_key. Anonymous viewers have is_authenticated=False which blocks T2 field access (defense-in-depth). Members bypass T2 entirely; T3 requires RBAC permission check.

entries.title: Visibility System — fix profile endpoint gap + 26 cross-app integration tests
entries.summary: Fixed BusinessProfileView.get() missing visibility context (T2 fields were not filtered on /business/{slug}/profile/ endpoint). Added 26 cross-app integration tests covering: cross-endpoint consistency (detail/by-id/profile), PATCH override persistence (override → detail reflects), is_public toggle (T2 hide/show), membership T3 visibility (owner/base-member/permissioned-member/non-member), is_limited flag consistency (6 viewer types), T2 override boundary conditions (exact-level/above-level/anonymous guard). Total: 3564 passed, 314 skipped.
entries.critical: BusinessProfileView.get() was missing VisibilityResolver.compute_viewer_access() — T2 contact fields were not filtered on profile endpoint. The mixin falls through when no visibility context is passed.

entries.title: Frontend Network System — Follow, Connect & My Network (43 new tests)
entries.summary: Implemented full frontend network system: smart CTA buttons (FollowButton with 3 states, ConnectButton with 4 states including Accept/Decline for incoming requests), My Network page with Connections/Following tabs and search, business console network management pages (followers, connections) with RBAC-gated actions. Added backend tweaks for viewer_role, follow_id, connection_id in _relationship responses. 43 new frontend tests (7 files) + 7 backend tests. Types, API (13 functions), query keys, 6 query hooks, 7 mutation hooks, 2 status configs, navigation config updates. Total: 1126 frontend tests, 532 backend tests for affected apps.

entries.title: Network System — Implementation documentation (backend v2 + frontend v1)
entries.summary: Updated backend implementation doc to v2 (added viewer_role, follow_id, connection_id to _relationship section, updated modified files, added 7 relationship tests to count, updated vNext removing completed items, added v2 changelog). Created comprehensive frontend implementation doc covering: architecture overview, 13 API functions with input types, 6 query keys, 6 types, 6 query hooks + 7 mutation hooks, FollowButton (3-state) + ConnectButton (4-state) CTAs with full state machines, 3 page components, profile integration on 3 routes, 5 key flows, navigation config, 50 total tests (43 frontend + 7 backend), 22 new files + 11 modified files, 6 gotchas, 3 known limitations.

entries.title: Network System — E2E testing + 3 bug fixes
entries.summary: Executed 33 E2E tests for Network system (Follow, Connect, My Network, Business Console, Cross-scope) via MCP stealth browser. All passed. Fixed 3 bugs: (1) serializers._resolve_followee_name() used biz.name/plat.name which don't exist on the account models - fixed to query profile models (biz.profile.display_name, plat.profile.name) with select_related; (2) UserPublicDetailView._build_relationship_data() filtered connection transactions incorrectly for target users - fixed to check both initiator_id and target_id; (3) frontend useRemoveBusinessFollower mutation only invalidated businessFollowers query, not businessStats - added stats invalidation so follower count updates immediately after removal. Updated impl doc to v3.
